vehicles.name: British Rail Class 68 (Cross Country)
vehicles.description: The Class 68 is a type of mainline mixed traffic diesel-electric locomotive manufactured by Stadler Rail Valencia (and previously by Vossloh España) for Direct Rail Services (DRS) in the United Kingdom. The design is derived from the Stadler Eurolight, and Stadler's product name for this variant is the UKLight.

vehicles.name: SNCB I10 InterCity (IC) Coach (Trailer)
vehicles.description: The Eurofima coach, or European Standard Coach (French: Voiture Standard Européenne), is a passenger car designed for use on international railway routes in Europe. It was commissioned by the European Company for the Financing of Railroad Rolling Stock (French: Société européenne pour le financement de matériel ferroviaire).

Class 142 - Pacer (Forwards)
vehicles.description: Class 142 diesel multiple unit passenger trains were built for British Rail (BR) from 1985 to 1987, with a high level of commonality with the widely used Leyland National bus. They are part of the Pacer family of railbuses. The last set was withdrawn from service in 2020.

Waterline Class 730 Cab (Forwards)
vehicles.description: The British Rail Class 730/0 Aventra is an electric multiple-unit designed by Bombardier Transportation and manufactured by Alstom, in use on Waterline. These trains are formed of 3 coaches, with a max speed of 90 MPH, and cost 1,000 points.

vehicles.name: Stepford Connect Class 730 Cab (Forwards)

vehicles.name: SCR Stepford Express Class 43 (HST) Cab (Forwards)
vehicles.description: The British Rail Class 43 (HST), also known as the InterCity 125 High Speed Train, is a diesel-electric locomotive manufactured by BREL, in use on Stepford Express. Two of these locomotives (also referred to as powercars) top-and-tailing four Mark 3 coaches can be purchased for 3,000 points, while the variant with 5 Mark 3s and the variant with powercars with added cosmetic buffers each cost an additional 800 points. When this train first came out, it was priced at only 2,400 points until 26 December 2019.

vehicles.name: SCR Stepford Express Class 43 (HST) Cab (Backwards)
vehicles.description: The British Rail Class 43 (HST), also known as the InterCity 125 High Speed Train, is a diesel-electric locomotive manufactured by BREL, in use on Stepford Express. Two of these locomotives (also referred to as powercars) top-and-tailing four Mark 3 coaches can be purchased for 3,000 points, while the variant with 5 Mark 3s and the variant with powercars with added cosmetic buffers each cost an additional 800 points. When this train first came out, it was priced at only 2,400 points until 26 December 2019.

vehicles.name: SCR Stepford Express Class 43 (HST) (Trailer)
vehicles.description: The British Rail Class 43 (HST), also known as the InterCity 125 High Speed Train, is a diesel-electric locomotive manufactured by BREL, in use on Stepford Express. Two of these locomotives (also referred to as powercars) top-and-tailing four Mark 3 coaches can be purchased for 3,000 points, while the variant with 5 Mark 3s and the variant with powercars with added cosmetic buffers each cost an additional 800 points. When this train first came out, it was priced at only 2,400 points until 26 December 2019.

vehicles.name: Stepford Connect Class 68 Coaches (1st Class) (Trailer)
vehicles.description: The Mark 3 Coaches are non-powered passenger rail vehicles used in SCR for services on Stepford Connect in rakes of 3 coaches and a Class 68 locomotive on each end and on Stepford Express in rakes of 4, 5, 7, 8 or 9 (one of them being a First Class only coach) with a Class 43 locomotive on each end. They are NGv3 with paper PIS on the outside and a scrolling PIS on the inside.

vehicles.name: Stepford Connect Class 68 Coaches (2nd Class) (Trailer)
vehicles.description: The Mark 3 Coaches are non-powered passenger rail vehicles used in SCR for services on Stepford Connect in rakes of 3 coaches and a Class 68 locomotive on each end and on Stepford Express in rakes of 4, 5, 7, 8 or 9 (one of them being a First Class only coach) with a Class 43 locomotive on each end. They are NGv3 with paper PIS on the outside and a scrolling PIS on the inside.

vehicles.name: mark 5a first class (Trailer)
vehicles.description: The British Rail Mark 5A is a type of railway vehicle in use in the UK. 52 standard carriages and 14 driving trailers were built by Spanish manufacturer CAF, and were operated by TransPennine Express. They first entered service on 24 August 2019, with the last set being withdrawn with the timetable change occurring on 10 December 2023.

vehicles.description: The British Rail Mark 5A is a type of railway vehicle in use in the UK. 52 standard carriages and 14 driving trailers were built by Spanish manufacturer CAF, and were operated by TransPennine Express. They first entered service on 24 August 2019, with the last set being withdrawn with the timetable change occurring on 10 December 2023.

vehicles.description:
The Class 134 ‘DM90’ was a Diesel Multiple Unit manufactured by Travtrans Moszya-Regalia between 1996 and 1998. 
Top speed: 140 km/h.
Manufacturer:TMR.
Manufactured Between: 1996-1998.
Power Output: 860Hp.
Power: Diesel.
Engine: Khammnos 1996 V6. Coaches: 2.

Moszyan Railways C73 DDM Coach
vehicles.description: The C73 Coach is a Double Decker Intercity Passenger coach Manufactured by Travtrans Moszya-Regalia for Moszyan Railways and were put into service in 1994. Starting in 1998 and ending in 2009, they were converted into Class 475 and 476 units.

vehicles.name: Moszyan Railways Class 475 DDAR Trailer
vehicles.description: The Class 475 and 476 'DDZ' are classes of Electric Multiple Units manufactured by Travtrans Moszya-Regalia for Moszyan Railways as the C73 and C74 coaches. They were manufactured between 1993 and 1995, and entered service in 1994. Between 1998 and 2002, They were converted to 4-car EMUs, named the Class 475. The equipment was placed in the first coach, taking up the lower deck, making the coach the only Bo’+2’+Bo’ units. In 2009, the units were refurbished in Moszya, and some were converted into the 6-car units, named the Class 476.

vehicles.name: Moszyan Railways Class 68
vehicles.description: The Class 68 is a type of mainline mixed traffic diesel-electric locomotive manufactured by Stadler Rail Valencia (and previously by Vossloh España) for Direct Rail Services (DRS) in the United Kingdom. The design is derived from the Stadler Eurolight, and Stadler's product name for this variant is the UKLight.

Spawn Rail Xplorer DMU (Forwards)
vehicles.description: Xplorer is a DMU introduced in the state of NSW in 1993. It operates mainly on long - distance routes within the state and operates from Sydney to Broken Hill, Armidale, Griffith, Moree, and Canberra. This train was produced by ABB Transportation.
